mechanism_narrative: Industrial production tends to trend upward during expansions; a sustained positive trend in INDPRO signals healthy supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO trend signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the strategy produces negative average returns in 60-day windows following rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate indicates weakening labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by the trigger amount but the mean 20-day forward return is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p>0.05) over at least 10 occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer and investment demand, supporting corporate profits and pushing equity prices higher over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS are above their respective 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: When both series are above their moving averages, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns is less than 55% over a minimum of 15 occurrences, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates tighten financial conditions, raise discount rates on future cash flows, and typically reduce equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is above its trailing 12-month average and the mean 20-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over at least 2 years of data.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in retail sales suggests a sudden boost in consumer demand, which can drive short-term positive sentiment and upward price pressure on equities over the next week.
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met in at least 10 instances and the average 5-day forward return is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal a broadly strengthening economy with rising output and employment, which should support corporate earnings and lift stock prices over a one-month period.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS are above their respective 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: When both proxies are above their MAs, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences over a minimum of 15 instances, or the average return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: High and rising consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces central bank tightening, which depresses equity valuations over a longer horizon; the effect is strongest when CPI is above a threshold level.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is above 200, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns is greater than 50% over at least 10 observations, or the signal's Rank IC is not negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP signals that the economy is shrinking, leading to falling corporate earnings and broad market declines; the negative impact on equities is most pronounced over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Over at least 5 occurrences of negative quarterly GDP growth, the mean 60-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10).
